mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal broad-based economic strength and rising aggregate income, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and positive equity returns. This is a direct, always-on measure of economic health.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward information coefficient (IC) remains below 0.02 for a rolling 3-year period, indicating no predictive power for 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is near the zero lower bound, monetary policy has limited capacity to respond to negative shocks. This perceived lack of policy ammunition makes equities more vulnerable to adverse news, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are not statistically significantly lower (p > 0.05) during periods where FEDFUNDS < 0.25% compared to periods where it is higher.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where retail sales (RSAFS) are accelerating while housing starts (HOUST) are decelerating indicates that consumer spending is robust but the highly cyclical, credit-sensitive housing sector is weakening. This combination signals a potential late-cycle peak, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in RSAFS > 0 and 3-month change in HOUST < 0
failure_condition: Instances of the trigger condition are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recessionary environment, a strong rebound in industrial production (INDPRO) signals that the supply side of the economy is healing, restoring production capacity and driving operating leverage for industrial firms. This leads to upward earnings revisions and positive equity performance.
failure_condition: The correlation between 3-month changes in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative or statistically insignificant during periods following a >2pp rise in UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is a primary indicator of recession. When the 3-month change exceeds a critical threshold, it signals a sharp deterioration in the labor market, triggering fears of falling demand and causing a sell-off in equities.
evidence_refs: fact:The Sahm Rule uses a similar logic
failure_condition: Triggered instances are not followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of cases during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation, measured by the year-over-year change in CPI (CPIAUCSL), erodes the real value of future earnings and increases the probability of future monetary tightening by the central bank. Both effects increase the equity risk premium, leading to negative returns.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 12-month change in CPIAUCSL and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp, negative surprise in monthly housing starts (HOUST) has an immediate negative impact on market sentiment due to the sector's importance as a leading indicator for durable goods consumption and construction employment. This drives negative returns over a very short horizon.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a trigger event is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (GDPC1) is positive but the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is also rising, it signals a 'jobless recovery'. This indicates productivity gains are not translating into broad income growth, undermining consumer confidence and future aggregate demand, which is negative for equities.
candidate_trigger: Quarterly YoY % change in GDPC1 > 0 AND Quarterly change in UNRATE > 0
failure_condition: The S&P 500 posts positive average 60-day returns during quarters that satisfy the trigger condition over the historical sample.
